title: Rich Malaysian Chicken Curry
description: A fragrant, herbaceous curry infused with saffron and coconut milk, served in a stone bowl for a hearty meal-prep option.
instructions: Heat the oil in a stone bowl dolsot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the sliced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and ginger; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Sprinkle the turmeric, coriander, cumin, and chili flakes, stirring to coat the aromatics for 30 seconds.
Pour in the diced chicken, tossing to combine and letting it brown on all sides for 4 minutes.
Add the coconut milk, saffron strands, and low-sodium salt, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the dolsot, and let the curry cook for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Stir in the lime juice, taste, and adjust seasoning if needed.
Transfer the curry to a serving dish and garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
Serve hot with steamed rice or flatbread.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
